metadata.title: Build a Real-Time Verdict Module with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Hello. This is Liam, in for Bear. Fast enough on average is how safety-critical AI fails. The deadline you must never miss isn't the mean — it's the worst case.

props.command: claude "write realtime.py: inputs deadline_ms, deadline_class, measured_latencies[]. Compute WCET (tail, not mean), classify soft/firm/hard, emit verdict + pattern if in a hard loop."
props.segment: Build a Real-Time Verdict Module with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: From the book: hard, firm, and soft real-time compared on deadline tolerance and consequence of miss. Our verdict script classifies your system and checks whether the AI fits the timing envelope.

beats.act: MANIM — Histogram building: mean at 38 ms
beats.narration_text: Build the latency histogram from measured samples. Bars pile up around the mean — 38 milliseconds. The distribution looks good. Mean is well under the 50-millisecond deadline. Ship it? Not yet. The mean is not the metric that matters.

beats.act: MANIM — WCET tail extends past 50 ms deadline
beats.narration_text: Extend the histogram to its tail. The 95th percentile: 55 milliseconds — over the 50-millisecond deadline. The 99th percentile: 80 milliseconds. WCET — worst-case execution time — is the tail of the real distribution. One in a hundred inferences already misses the deadline. For a soft deadline, that might be acceptable. For a hard deadline, the device fails its safety case.

beats.act: MANIM — Jitter stall pushes tail to 120 ms
beats.narration_text: Add jitter. A garbage-collection pause or DMA interrupt introduces a stall. The worst-case tail extends to 120 milliseconds — 70 milliseconds past the deadline. This is the safety case everyone misses: the nominal distribution passes, but the jitter distribution fails. The real-time verdict must include jitter sources.

Your turn. Ask Claude: write realtime.py — inputs: deadline in milliseconds, deadline class, and a list of measured latencies. Compute WCET as the tail of the distribution, not the mean. Classify soft, firm, or hard deadline. Emit a verdict plus a pattern recommendation if running in a hard loop. WCET is the tail of the real distribution, not the mean. Add jitter from a GC stall or DMA interrupt and show the tail blow past the deadline — that is the safety case everyone misses.

props.command: claude "write realtime.py: inputs deadline_ms, deadline_class, measured_latencies[]. Compute WCET (tail, not mean), classify soft/firm/hard, emit verdict + pattern if in a hard loop."

props.title: Build a Real-Time Verdict Module with Claude Code
